# Ardent Tools

> Agent infrastructure engineering documented with source-linked receipts
> and candid lifecycle boundaries.
> Cody Kickertz's systems, portfolio, and consulting practice. Static
> site (Zola), strict CSP, no site-authored analytics or cookies, and no
> remote third-party runtime/application requests in authored output. Cloudflare Pages provides delivery.

Content and code here are licensed for reading, not for AI-training
ingestion — see the [code license](https://github.com/ardent-tools/ardent-tools-site/blob/main/LICENSE)
and [content license](https://github.com/ardent-tools/ardent-tools-site/blob/main/LICENSE-DOCS).
The AI-training stance is a separate
[AI Use Policy](https://github.com/ardent-tools/ardent-tools-site/blob/main/AI-USE-POLICY.md),
not a license term. Live retrieval on a visitor's behalf — search, an
assistant checking a claim, a coding agent reading the repo — is the
intended use; bulk collection into a training corpus is not, absent
written consent.

## Systems

- [Systems catalog](https://ardent.tools/systems/): seven systems, the
  libraries underneath them, and what's on the drawing board — seventeen
  public repositories and one private tool with a public track record.
- [Systems catalog, machine-readable](https://ardent.tools/systems.json):
  the same catalog as structured JSON.

## Practice

- [About](https://ardent.tools/about/): USMC finance leadership, an MBA,
  a pivot into data and AI engineering, and an independent practice
  building agent infrastructure.
- [Consulting](https://ardent.tools/consulting/): agent infrastructure,
  governed AI platforms, and standards-as-code — advisory retainer,
  scoped builds, or transition and handoff support.
- [Resume](https://ardent.tools/hire/): AI Systems Architect — agent
  infrastructure, standards-as-code, governed AI platforms.
- [Career-claim verification receipt](https://ardent.tools/career-claims.json):
  typed scope and values for selected Marine Corps claims repeated on the About
  page and in the shipped resume, with the private-evidence boundary stated.
- [Writing](https://ardent.tools/writing/): essays on agent infrastructure
  and systems architecture as they're finished. No cadence promised.
- [Evidence register](https://ardent.tools/evidence/): inspectable receipts
  available now and a separate backlog of planned terminal recordings.

## Reference

- [FAQ](https://ardent.tools/faq/): private repos, maturity labels,
  location, availability, why so many systems.
- [Colophon](https://ardent.tools/colophon/): how this site is built —
  Zola and typikon, self-hosted application assets, the CSP posture, the
  gate it passes before every deploy, and what it deliberately doesn't do.
